subject: Re: Jerry Goldsmith
body:
tell jerry , we didn
t know it was a s corp.... we are researching it

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I spoke with Jerry Goldsmith, who told me that you
> promised a $200,000  
> subscription to purchase 20,000 shares of common
> stock of First Bank of the Palm  
> Beaches at $10.00 per share, and asked me to Fed Ex
> him for delivery tomorrow  
> the subscription documents, along with a $200,000
> check payable to the order 
> of  Nexity Bank, Escrow Agent.
>  
> Your 20,000 share subscription will constitute less
> than 5% of outstanding  
> shares if the minimum number of shares are
> subscribed for in this offering  
> (1,080,000).  
>  
> According to the offering circular, it is intended
> that the Bank will make  
> an S election and each subscriber is being asked to
> sign a Federal S  Election. 
>  This obviously limits which of your entities can
> make  the subscription 
> (e.g., no non-US citizens, no C corps, no S Corps,
> no  partnerships and no LLCs 
> and only QSSTs).  As a result,  it is likely that
> you would have to make this 
> investment in your individual  capacity.
>  
> According to the offering circular, it is intended
> that tax distributions  
> will be made, but there are no guarantees that there
> will be tax  distributions.
>  
> Did you agree to this subscription? 
>  
> If so, may I subscribe for the 20,000 shares in your
> name and may I  
> authorize a $200,000 check to be cut from your
> personal account?  May I  send the 
> subscription docs and the check to Jerry by Fed Ex
> for tomorrow  delivery?
>  
> Please advise.
>  
> Darren
> 



		
__________________________________

body_html:
tell jerry , we didn
t know it was a s corp.... we are researching it

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I spoke with Jerry Goldsmith, who told me that you
promised a $200,000
subscription to purchase 20,000 shares of common
stock of First Bank of the Palm
Beaches at $10.00 per share, and asked me to Fed Ex
him for delivery tomorrow
the subscription documents, along with a $200,000
check payable to the order
of Nexity Bank, Escrow Agent.

Your 20,000 share subscription will constitute less
than 5% of outstanding
shares if the minimum number of shares are
subscribed for in this offering
(1,080,000).

According to the offering circular, it is intended
that the Bank will make
an S election and each subscriber is being asked to
sign a Federal S Election.
This obviously limits which of your entities can
make the subscription
(e.g., no non-US citizens, no C corps, no S Corps,
no partnerships and no LLCs
and only QSSTs). As a result, it is likely that
you would have to make this
investment in your individual capacity.

According to the offering circular, it is intended
that tax distributions
will be made, but there are no guarantees that there
will be tax distributions.

Did you agree to this subscription?

If so, may I subscribe for the 20,000 shares in your
name and may I
authorize a $200,000 check to be cut from your
personal account? May I send the
subscription docs and the check to Jerry by Fed Ex
for tomorrow delivery?

Please advise.

Darren

Yahoo! FareChase: Search multiple travel sites in one click.
http://farechase.yahoo.com
